lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is that cape you're wearing?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: It's a Skillcape of Smithing. It shows that I'm a master blacksmith, but that's only to be expected - after all, my ancestors were the greatest blacksmiths in dwarven history.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: If you ever achieve level 99 Smithing you'll be able to wear a cape like this, and receive more experience when smelting gold ore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make me another of Sir Vyvin's swords?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: You want that knight's sword again? I suppose you brought me a lovely pie, so I don't mind. I'll need a blurite ore and two iron bars, like before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll go and find that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you like a redberry pie?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I'd never say no to a redberry pie! We Imcando dwarves love them - they're GREAT!

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: By Guthix! THAT was good pie! Anyone who makes pie like THAT has got to be alright!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is that cape you're wearing?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: It's a Skillcape of Smithing. It shows that I'm a master blacksmith, but that's only to be expected - after all, my ancestors were the greatest blacksmiths in dwarven history.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: If you ever achieve level 99 Smithing you'll be able to wear a cape like this, and receive more experience when smelting gold ore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we talk about skillcapes?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: What do you need, human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now that I am so skilled at Smithing, can I buy a Skillcape of Smithing from you?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I reckon so; we master smiths must stick together! I'll give it to you for just 99000 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, that's too much money.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Too much money? A smith of your calibre should be able to make that amount in an hour!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course I'll pay you.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: All Skillcapes come with a free hood. It's part of a deal: buy one get one free, you know. So you'll need to free up some inventory space before I can sell you one.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Excellent! Wear that cape with pride my friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've met another Imcando dwarf.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I very much doubt that [player name]. I'm the last Imcando dwarf left in Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No it's true. I was accidentally helping some grave robbers unearth some treasure below Ice Mountain. Long story short, it turned out to be the ruins of Camdozaal.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Camdozaal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I don't believe me ears! Tell me what else you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well there's not much left there really. It's just a ruin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But there's a lone Imcando dwarf keeping it alive. His name is Ramarno.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Ramarno...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's managed to keep the city going all these years, even with his lack of vision.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Thank you for bringing me this news [player name]. I'll need to make some time to see the city with my own eyes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you make of this, Thurgo?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Hmph, who wrote this text? It seems new.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I-

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Nevermind! If you ever plan on going back to this island, I'll happily come along.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... Umm... Err...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you believe it could possibly lead to the ancient ruins of your race?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I'll believe it when I see it... Now begone, I've got things to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, Thurgo. I'll see you later.

sections.title: Delivering a letter from the Wise Old Man

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Wise Old Man says he's got the information you wanted. Here's his message.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Ooh, thanks. I've been hoping he'd send me that information soon, although I wouldn't mind if he'd send me a pie instead!

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I'd give you a reward, but you don't seem to have any space for it. Come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Some guy died down in the hole just north of my house, and he dropped these herbs. Here, take them.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Here's some cash for your time.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I found this while I was mining. Hope you like it.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Magic isn't really my thing. Here, take these.
